senses.definition: The correct arrangement of parts in relation to each other.
examples: The mechanic checked the alignment of the wheels.
Bad chair alignment can cause back pain.
The dentist corrected the alignment of her teeth.

senses.definition: Support for or agreement with a person, group, or idea.
examples: The company announced its alignment with international safety standards.
Political alignment can change over time.
Their values showed a strong alignment with our mission.

senses.definition: Different ways in which things or people are arranged or in agreement with each other.
examples: The project requires several alignments between departments.
Various political alignments appeared after the election.
The engineer tested different alignments of the parts.

senses.definition: Places something in a straight line or in the correct position.
examples: The software automatically aligns the text on the page.
She carefully aligns the pictures on the wall.
The machine aligns the parts before cutting them.

senses.definition: Agrees or matches with an idea, rule, or belief.
examples: The policy aligns with international standards.
His opinion aligns closely with mine.
The plan aligns with our long‑term goals.

senses.definition: A decorative Greek pattern made of a continuous line that bends in repeated right angles.
examples: The border of the ceiling was decorated with an aligreek design.
The architect chose an aligreek ornament for the frieze.
Ancient buildings often used aligreek patterns in stone carvings.

senses.definition: A Latin word meaning 'and others', used especially in lists of people in academic writing.
examples: The paper by Smith et alii is often cited in this field.
The decision in Brown et alii changed the legal view.
The study by Jones et alii influenced later research.

senses.definition: A tropical plant, Canna indica, grown for its bright flowers and large leaves.
examples: The garden includes an aliipoe near the pond.
Farmers in some regions grow aliipoe as an ornamental plant.
The red flowers of the aliipoe stand out in the field.

senses.definition: Very similar in appearance, character, or amount.
examples: The two sisters look very alike.
Their ideas are surprisingly alike.
These dishes taste alike to me.

senses.definition: In the same way or to the same degree.
examples: The rule applies to everyone alike.
Young and old alike enjoyed the show.
The law protects workers and employers alike.

senses.definition: The state of being similar to someone or something.
examples: There is a strong alikeness between the two paintings.
The alikeness of their views surprised their friends.
He was struck by the alikeness between the twins.

senses.definition: Different ways or cases in which things are similar.
examples: They discussed the alikenesses and differences between the two systems.
Many alikenesses can be found in their stories.
We must pay attention to both alikenesses and contrasts.

senses.definition: In the same way; similarly (nonstandard or rare form of 'likewise').
examples: He spoke softly, and she alikewise lowered her voice.
The first group finished late, and the second alikewise took extra time.
Children and adults alikewise enjoyed the performance.

senses.definition: An older name recorded for the Kawésqar people or their language of southern Chile.
examples: Some early sources refer to the community as Alikuluf.
The Alikuluf language has very few speakers left.
Researchers studied traditional Alikuluf culture and stories.

senses.definition: Relating to the Alikuluf people, their language, or their culture.
examples: She specializes in Alikulufan history.
The museum has an exhibit of Alikulufan tools.
They documented Alikulufan traditions in a report.

senses.definition: A less common form of the word 'alalonga', used in specific historical or regional contexts.
examples: The old document used the spelling alilonghi.
Scholars note that alilonghi appears only in a few texts.
The term alilonghi is rarely found in modern writing.

senses.definition: A female given name, especially in Arabic‑speaking or Muslim communities.
examples: Alima welcomed us into her home.
They named their daughter Alima.
Alima is a popular name in some regions.

senses.definition: An uncommon or incorrect form of 'alimentation', meaning the act of giving food or nourishment.
examples: The report mistakenly used alimenation instead of alimentation.
Alimenation here refers to the supply of food.
The text discusses alimenation and health.

senses.definition: Food that keeps a person or animal alive and healthy.
examples: Rice is a basic aliment in many countries.
They searched the forest for any kind of aliment.
Bread and milk served as their main aliment.

senses.definition: To give food or support to someone or something.
examples: The rich soil alimented the crops.
Regular meals aliment the growing child.
Fresh grass alimented the cattle.

senses.definition: Related to food or nourishment.
examples: The report focused on the patient's alimental needs.
They studied the alimental value of different grains.
Good alimental habits are important for children.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to food or nourishment.
examples: The two diets are alimentally similar.
The dishes differ only alimentally, not in appearance.
The plan is sound both medically and alimentally.

senses.definition: The quality of providing nourishment.
examples: They evaluated the alimentariness of the new food product.
Alimentariness is essential when designing emergency rations.
The soup's high alimentariness made it suitable for patients.

senses.definition: Related to food, digestion, or the organs that deal with food.
examples: The doctor diagnosed an alimentary disorder.
Alimentary habits can affect overall health.
He studied the alimentary canal in biology class.

senses.definition: The process of giving or receiving food and nourishment.
examples: Proper alimentation is vital for recovery.
The study focused on infant alimentation.
They discussed the alimentation of patients in intensive care.

senses.definition: Different processes or methods of giving or receiving food and nourishment.
examples: The clinic offers several types of infant alimentations.
Various artificial alimentations are used in hospitals.
They compared natural and medical alimentations.

senses.definition: Serving to nourish or support life.
examples: The soup has strong alimentative qualities.
They chose foods with high alimentative value.
Alimentative substances are important during illness.

senses.definition: In a way that provides nourishment or support.
examples: The diet is alimentatively sufficient.
These foods function alimentatively rather than just as treats.
The formula is alimentatively balanced for infants.

senses.definition: The degree to which something can nourish.
examples: They measured the alimentativeness of the new supplement.
The alimentativeness of the meal was questioned.
High alimentativeness is needed in emergency rations.

senses.definition: Gave food or nourishment to someone or something.
examples: The simple diet alimented the patients during recovery.
Warm milk and bread alimented the hungry child.
Fresh grass alimented the herd through the winter.

senses.definition: A person or thing that provides food or nourishment.
examples: The program acts as an alimenter for poor families.
The river is an important alimenter of the wetlands.
The charity became a key alimenter in the region.

senses.definition: Related to food or nourishment (rare).
examples: They studied the alimentic effects of the new formula.
The report lists the alimentic properties of several plants.
Alimentic concerns guided their choice of menu.

senses.definition: Giving food or nourishment to someone or something.
examples: They focused on alimenting the refugees.
The nurse is responsible for alimenting the weakest patients.
Alimenting the herd during drought was difficult.

senses.definition: Providing nourishment or related to nourishment.
examples: The alimentive content of the meal was high.
They examined the alimentive qualities of the grain.
Alimentive factors must be considered in long trips.

senses.definition: The ability of something to nourish or support life.
examples: They compared the alimentiveness of several diets.
High alimentiveness is required in hospital meals.
The alimentiveness of the new product impressed the researchers.

senses.definition: The treatment of disease by carefully controlled diet.
examples: The clinic specializes in alimentotherapy for diabetes.
Alimentotherapy can reduce the need for some medicines.
They chose alimentotherapy as part of the treatment plan.

senses.definition: Foods that nourish people or animals.
examples: They stored various aliments for the winter.
Basic aliments such as rice and beans were distributed.
The market sells fresh aliments every day.

senses.definition: Food or nourishment, especially in older or scientific use.
examples: The text refers to water as an essential alimentum.
Ancient writers spoke of bread as the chief alimentum.
Researchers described milk as a complete alimentum for infants.

senses.definition: Receiving money as alimony after a separation or divorce.
examples: She became an alimonied spouse after the court decision.
The alimonied partner moved to a smaller apartment.
An alimonied parent may still face financial stress.

senses.definition: Regular money payments ordered by a court to support a former husband or wife.
examples: The court set high alimonies after the divorce.
He fell behind on his alimonies.
Different countries treat alimonies in different ways.

senses.definition: Money that a person must pay regularly to a former husband or wife after a divorce.
examples: He pays alimony to his ex‑wife every month.
The judge increased the amount of alimony.
She relies on alimony to cover her rent.

senses.definition: A given name used for boys or girls in various cultures.
examples: Alin moved to the city last year.
They named their son Alin.
Alin works as a software engineer.

senses.definition: Relating to the wings of the nose.
examples: The surgeon noted slight alinasal swelling.
Alinasal veins can sometimes be seen on the skin.
The patient reported pain in the alinasal region.

senses.definition: To place things in a straight line or in the correct position.
examples: Please aline the text in the center of the page.
The workers carefully alined the pipes.
She alined the photos along the wall.

senses.definition: The act of placing things in a line or in the correct position.
examples: The engineer is responsible for the alineation of the parts.
Careful alineation of the beams is necessary.
Errors in alineation can cause serious problems.

senses.definition: Different acts or instances of placing things in line or correct position.
examples: Repeated alineations were needed during construction.
The machine performs several alineations each hour.
Various alineations of the device were tested.

senses.definition: Placed things in a straight line or into the correct position.
examples: They alined the chairs in neat rows.
The mechanic alined the wheels yesterday.
She alined the columns in the document.

senses.definition: The arrangement of things or people in a line or in the correct relative positions.
examples: The alinement of the stones suggests careful planning.
Engineers checked the alinement of the bridge supports.
Bad alinement can damage the machine.

senses.definition: Different arrangements of things or people in a line or correct positions.
examples: The architect considered several possible alinements.
Different alinements of the pillars were tested.
They compared the alinements used in ancient structures.

senses.definition: A person or device that lines things up or puts them in the correct position.
examples: He worked as an aliner on the production line.
The machine acts as an automatic aliner for the bottles.
Each aliner in the team has a specific task.

senses.definition: People or devices that line things up or put them in the correct position.
examples: Several alinors and alin ers worked on the new machine line.
The factory installed two new alinors and alin ers.
The team of alinors and alin ers improved the process.

senses.definition: Puts things in a straight line or into the correct position.
examples: She alines the text to the left.
The worker alines each piece before welding.
The program automatically alines the images.

senses.definition: Lacking a tongue, or relating to the absence of a tongue.
examples: The surgeon described the patient as alingual after the operation.
Alingual animals face special feeding challenges.
The condition is rare and usually not completely alingual.

senses.definition: Placing things in a straight line or into the correct position.
examples: The workers are alining the rails.
Alining the text takes only a few clicks.
She spent the afternoon alining the chairs.
